work_unit: xiaozhi_server WAV/PCM size cap (PA-AUD-H-002 derived fix)
situation: audit-2026-07-08-042 finding PA-AUD-H-002: xiaozhi_server.py:171 _asr() writes wav_bytes to disk via tempfile.mkstemp with no upper-bound check. Malicious or buggy xiaozhi device can stream multi-GB audio across listen:start→stop window → server disk-fill DoS. Symmetrically, _on_audio accumulates PCM in memory without cap → OOM if device never sends listen:stop.
action: Added two module-level constants MAX_WAV_BYTES=MAX_PCM_BYTES=50MB (~26min 16kHz/16bit mono, far above any real utterance). Inserted PCM cap guard at top of _on_audio that flips self._aborted and logs warning when exceeded. Inserted WAV cap guard in _finalize_utterance between _to_wav() and _asr() call that drops oversized utterance with warning log instead of writing to disk.
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh committed + pushed auto/opt-xiaozhi-1783385638 to origin; git show confirms MAX_WAV_BYTES/MAX_PCM_BYTES constants + _on_audio PCM cap branch + _finalize_utterance WAV cap branch present on worktree branch. Main checkout reverted (expected post-migration).
next_suggestion: Next pending derived_fix by severity: PA-AUD-M-001 (audio_params unsanitized sample_rate → frame_size explosion DoS, same file). Then M-002 (discard threshold uses wrong rate), M-003 (proactive log injection), L-001 (JSON parse silent), L-002 (transcript unbounded read). All route-fix class, all single-file except M-003 targets proactive.py.

audit_findings.finding: _asr() writes arbitrary-size wav_bytes to tempfile.mkstemp without upper bound → disk-fill DoS vector from malicious/buggy xiaozhi device
audit_findings.remediation: Add MAX_WAV_BYTES constant (50MB); check len(wav_bytes) before write; drop + warn if exceeded

audit_findings.finding: _on_audio extends _pcm_buf without memory cap → OOM if device streams indefinitely without listen:stop
audit_findings.remediation: Add MAX_PCM_BYTES constant; check len(_pcm_buf) before extend; abort utterance + warn if exceeded
